opportunities.ai_thesis: Heuristic enhancement (Claude unavailable)

The Trade Desk's newly announced OpenAI advertising integration partnership represents a structural re-rating catalyst, positioning TTD as the programmatic layer for AI-driven ad targeting at a moment when the stock sits 70% below its 52-week high — offering an asymmetric entry rarely seen in high-quality adtech. CEO Jeff Green's $148M personal share purchase immediately following the announcement is the strongest possible insider signal, corroborated by four insider buys in 90 days, indicating management views current prices as deeply undervalued. With StockTwits sentiment at 91% bullish and a score of 106, momentum is inflecting now — buy TTD at $27.66 before the partnership details drive institutional re-accumulation.

SoFi Technologies presents a compelling contrarian entry following a 32.8% drawdown from its 52-week high, with an analyst upgrade to "buy" signaling that valuation has reset to levels attractive relative to fintech peers. Trading at $18.29 — 44% below its peak — the technical dislocation combined with 100% bullish retail sentiment on StockTwits creates asymmetric upside as the upgrade catalyst drives institutional re-rating. Despite muted insider buying, the risk/reward favors initiating a position now before consensus catches up to the revised valuation thesis.

IREN's planned acquisition of 50,000 GPUs positions it to capture a $3.7B AI Cloud ARR target by end-2026, with the catalyst expected to materialize within 62 days as the AI infrastructure build-out accelerates. At $38.12 — 50% below its 52-week high — the stock offers a compelling mean-reversion entry backed by 94% bullish retail sentiment and an 84/100 quantitative score, suggesting the selloff has been overdone relative to the underlying business trajectory. With neutral insider activity removing the overhang of distribution risk, this is a defined-risk entry ahead of a concrete, near-term expansion catalyst.

Alamos Gold (AGI) is entering a sentiment inflection point as Goldman Sachs initiates coverage with a BUY and BofA raises its price target to $57, while the company's newly announced dividend hike signals management confidence in sustained free cash flow from its Brazil loan operations. At $50.56 — 9% below the 52-week high — AGI offers a technically clean entry before a potential retest of highs, with institutional accumulation by Van ECK and 100% bullish StockTwits sentiment confirming the momentum setup. The partnership-driven catalyst with near-term analyst price target convergence around $57 implies ~13% upside with improving capital allocation as the fundamental anchor.

Occidental Petroleum's analyst consensus fair value has been revised upward to $52.52 with updated 2026 guidance and constructive oil price assumptions driving price targets into the mid-to-high $50s, yet the stock trades at $53.12 — offering a compelling risk/reward as near-term macro headwinds (G-7 reserve releases, Iran supply relief) create a discounted entry point ahead of that fundamental re-rating. Insider buying and 85% bullish retail sentiment confirm accumulation at current levels, while the stock sitting just 6% off its 52-week high signals underlying technical resilience rather than distribution. With a composite score of 117 well above the BUY threshold, initiate a position now to capture the gap to the $55-58 target range as 2026 earnings visibility improves.

POET Technologies presents a compelling entry ahead of OFC 2026 (62 days out), where its hybrid laser and next-gen AI light source showcase could serve as a significant re-rating catalyst for its optical interposer platform targeting AI data center infrastructure. At $7.00 — 26% below the 52-week high — the stock offers a discounted entry with asymmetric upside into a high-conviction event, supported by exceptional retail momentum (93% bullish sentiment, score 102/100) before institutional attention fully arrives. Neutral insider activity removes near-term distribution risk, making this a clean technical setup to accumulate before the conference narrative builds.

CoreWeave's partnership with Perplexity validates its AI infrastructure positioning and catalyzed a 6% pre-market surge that has begun reversing post-earnings weakness, with the full impact expected to materialize over the next 65 days as the deal terms become clearer. At $74.92 — 60% below its 52-week high — the risk/reward is asymmetric: the stock is deeply oversold while StockTwits sentiment sits at 100% bullish and the new Flexible Capacity Plans (Flex Reservations + Spot) expand CoreWeave's addressable customer base beyond hyperscale commitments. The absence of insider buying is a watch item, but the combination of a confirmed enterprise catalyst, depressed valuation, and fresh product optionality makes this a high-conviction entry ahead of the 65-day catalyst window.

DSKYF presents a compelling entry at $18.36 (37% below its 52-week high) ahead of its exclusive GAIA/Daiichi Sankyo Europe digital therapeutic partnership launching across European cardiovascular markets within 83 days — a high-conviction catalyst with a 98/100 score. Neutral insider activity and only 50% bullish sentiment on StockTwits signal the market has not yet fully priced in this partnership, leaving significant upside as institutional awareness builds into the commercialization announcement. Buy now while sentiment is subdued and price remains deeply discounted, targeting a re-rate toward prior highs as the European launch timeline compresses.

Adobe's expanded MLB partnership signals accelerating enterprise AI adoption, positioning the company to capture recurring revenue from AI-driven content workflows at scale — a catalyst that the market has yet to fully price in. At $275, ADBE trades 38% below its 52-week high with 71% bullish retail sentiment and a 91/100 score, offering a technically compressed entry into a high-quality compounder before institutional re-rating. Negative insider activity warrants a tight stop, but the risk/reward favors initiation here given the AI partnership tailwind and depressed valuation relative to Adobe's durable competitive moat in creative software.

Arcutis Biotherapeutics (ARQT) presents a compelling entry point ahead of anticipated Phase 3 open-label extension data for ZORYVE cream in pediatric atopic dermatitis (ages 2-5) expected within ~110 days, a label expansion that would meaningfully broaden the addressable market for its flagship asset. Trading 23% below its 52-week high with a score of 119/100, the stock offers technical value at $24.59 while retail sentiment is uniformly bullish — and the absence of insider selling (rather than buying) in a post-profitability, guidance-raised company suggests management confidence without the noise of near-term distribution. The convergence of a de-risked balance sheet, raised 2026 guidance, and a binary pediatric catalyst makes this an asymmetric risk/reward setup before the data readout.

UiPath's first-mover AIUC-1 certification positions it as the regulatory standard-bearer for enterprise AI agents, with a Q4 earnings catalyst ~77 days out expected to validate commercial traction from its new agentic AI offerings. At $11.59 — 42% off its 52-week high — the stock offers asymmetric upside as 94% bullish retail sentiment and a perfect 100/100 score signal the market is beginning to reprice this regulatory moat ahead of earnings. Negative insider activity warrants a measured position, but the combination of a hard-to-replicate safety certification and deeply discounted entry makes this a compelling risk/reward setup into the catalyst window.

ServiceNow presents a compelling contrarian entry at $116.61 — 45% below its 52-week high — where analyst price target cuts have created an oversold dislocation that insiders are quietly exploiting with 2 buys in the past 90 days. The negative catalyst (target cuts driving retail exits) appears largely priced in given the stock's severe drawdown, while StockTwits sentiment at 93% bullish signals retail conviction is holding despite institutional noise, creating a potential floor. With a $123B market cap and moderate insider accumulation confirming management's confidence, the risk/reward favors initiating a position here ahead of sentiment normalization.

Insider buying activity suggests company management sees value at these depressed levels, indicating potential mean reversion. However, without a concrete catalyst to reignite market interest, this remains a speculative contrarian bet on recovery rather than a high-conviction opportunity.

Ligand Pharmaceuticals (LGND) scores 92/100 on our model following a Q4 2025 earnings beat on March 4, with the stock consolidating just 2% below its 52-week high of ~$213 — a technically constructive setup that often precedes breakout continuation. The QTORIN royalty growth narrative is gaining momentum with StockTwits sentiment at 100% bullish, and a 33-day window to the next catalyst creates an asymmetric entry before broader institutional recognition. Despite negative insider activity (a risk to monitor), the earnings beat thesis remains intact: initiate or add at current levels with a near-term target above the 52-week high.

events.summary: Ligand Pharmaceuticals has a PDUFA decision date for FILSPARI (sNDA) for focal segmental glomerulosclerosis treatment.
Ligand Pharmaceuticals' sNDA for FILSPARI in focal segmental glomerulosclerosis (FSGS) has PDUFA date of January 13, 2026.

Travere Therapeutics (TVTX) offers a compelling risk/reward setup ahead of the FDA's April 13 PDUFA date for sparsentan in FSGS — a rare kidney disease with limited treatment options — where approval would validate the company's lead asset and trigger a Mirum milestone payment atop accelerating FILSPARI revenue. At $28.63, shares trade 32% below their 52-week high with muted retail sentiment (57% bullish, near-neutral news flow) and no insider buying, creating a classic "wall of worry" entry point before a binary catalyst that Piper Sandler's raised price target suggests the Street is beginning to price in. With the PDUFA 33 days out and institutional positioning still cautious, the asymmetric setup favors initiating a position now before approval expectations compress the discount.

events.summary: Travere Therapeutics has a PDUFA decision date for FILSPARI (sNDA) for focal segmental glomerulosclerosis treatment.
Travere Therapeutics has a PDUFA date for sNDA of FILSPARI for focal segmental glomerulosclerosis on January 13, 2026.

opportunities.ai_thesis: DocuSign presents a compelling entry ahead of its Q4 FY2026 earnings on March 17, 2026 — just 6 days away — where the recently announced Anthropic Cowork partnership is expected to feature prominently, providing a dual catalyst that has already driven a surge in retail conviction (91% bullish, sentiment +0.41). At $48.64, shares trade 49% below their 52-week high, offering asymmetric upside with a $9.7B market cap that appears to undervalue the AI-enhanced contract intelligence opportunity. While zero insider buys in 90 days warrants monitoring, the combination of a near-term earnings event, a high-profile AI partnership, and deeply discounted technicals makes the risk/reward favorable for a pre-earnings position.
VRDN has a potential FDA catalyst 111 days out, but with no details or supporting narrative, execution risk is high. The 15% decline and insider selling suggest limited conviction ahead of the event.

VERA presents a compelling risk/reward setup with an FDA catalyst expected in ~118 days, offering a defined timeline for a potential binary event. At $40.25, the stock trades 28% below its 52-week high with neutral sentiment (50% bullish on StockTwits, flat news flow), suggesting the market has not yet priced in catalyst optimism — creating an asymmetric entry ahead of the event. Despite negative insider activity (1 buy in 90 days), the $2.9B market cap and pre-catalyst dislocation from highs make this an actionable BUY for risk-tolerant investors ahead of the FDA decision window.
Established industrial equipment manufacturer with $140B market cap providing stable, mature exposure; potential mean-reversion trade as the stock is down 12% from recent highs.

events.summary: Eaton, an industrial equipment manufacturer, is showing strong technical setups as industrials sector outpaces tech in early 2026.
